bids.reasoning: This is the opening bid at t=0, so I should start with my maximum utility offer. My most important issue is issueD (0.4 weight), followed by issueC (0.3), issueB (0.2), and issueA (0.1). For all issues, valueF gives 1.0 utility. I'll open with all valueF to maximize my utility at the start.

The opponent opened with their worst offer for me (all valueA, giving me 0.2 utility on each issue) and issued a blunt threat. This is classic aggressive anchoring - their message is cheap talk, and their bid is maximally self-serving. I opened with my best offer (all valueF). We're at t=0.02, very early in negotiation, so I should stay firm near my ideal but perhaps make a minimal concession to signal willingness to negotiate. I'll drop one low-weight issue slightly to show movement while keeping my high-priority issues (issueD, issueC) at valueF.

The opponent is making extreme demands (all valueA, which gives me minimum utility) and claiming they won't move, but this is clearly cheap talk at t=0.04 - we're only 4% through the negotiation. Their behavior (repeating the same extreme bid twice) shows they're holding firm early, which is typical. I should continue holding near my optimal position since there's no time pressure yet. I'll make a very small concession on issueA (lowest weight) while keeping my high-value issues at top values. My utility: 0.1*0.84 + 0.2*1.0 + 0.3*1.0 + 0.4*1.0 = 0.084 + 0.2 + 0.3 + 0.4 = 0.984.

We're very early (t=0.06), so I can afford to hold firm near my best offers. The opponent is being aggressive with "final offer" threats but has only barely conceded (issueD moved from valueA to valueB in their last offer), and their offer gives me extremely low utility (all A/B values). Their messages are classic cheap talk - threatening to walk away but still making (tiny) concessions. I should stay near my high-utility bids. I'll make a small concession on issueA (least important to me) while holding firm on issueD (most important). My previous bid was issueA: valueE, issueB: valueF, issueC: valueF, issueD: valueF - I'll keep it essentially the same but vary slightly to avoid exact repetition.

The opponent is making very aggressive threats ("final offer", "walk away") but their behavior contradicts this — they've been stuck at all-A bids with only a tiny concession on issueD (moved from valueA to valueB at t=0.06). This is classic cheap talk; they're not actually moving. We're only at t=0.08, very early in the negotiation. I should stay firm on my high-value issues (issueD and issueC, weights 0.4 and 0.3) while making small concessions on lower-weight issues. I'll slightly nudge issueB down while keeping issueC and issueD at valueF, and adjust issueA a bit more since it's lowest weight.

The opponent has been making near-identical offers (all valueA across the board) with very aggressive "final offer" rhetoric that contradicts their slow concession behavior - they've now moved issueD from valueA to valueB twice (at t=0.06 and t=0.08) and issueC from valueA to valueB (t=0.10). This suggests they are actually conceding slowly despite the threats. We're still early (t=0.10), so I can afford to hold firm on my most important issues (issueD, issueC) while making small concessions on less important ones (issueA, issueB). I'll concede slightly on issueB while keeping issueC and issueD at high values.

The opponent keeps making near-identical low offers (mostly valueA across everything) despite threatening to walk away. Their messages are pure cheap talk - they've been saying "final offer" since t=0.02 yet keep coming back. I'm only at t=0.12, so there's plenty of time. They've shown tiny movement: issueD went to valueB once (t=0.06), issueC went to valueB (t=0.10), but then reverted. My highest weights are issueD (0.4) and issueC (0.3), so I need to hold firm there. I'll make a modest concession on issueA and issueB (my lower-weight issues) while keeping issueC and issueD at high values. Let me try issueA: valueC, issueB: valueD, issueC: valueF, issueD: valueF - this gives me utility: 0.1*0.52 + 0.2*0.68 + 0.3*1.0 + 0.4*1.0 = 0.052 + 0.136 + 0.3 + 0.4 = 0.888.

The opponent keeps threatening to walk away but is actually making small concessions (issueC moved to valueB at t=0.10, issueD moved to valueC at t=0.14). Their messages are classic "cheap talk" intimidation. We're only at t=0.14, so there's plenty of time. The opponent's best offer to me so far gives low utility (issueD: valueC = 0.52*0.4 = 0.208, rest at valueA = very low). I should continue conceding slowly on low-importance issues while protecting issueD and issueC. I'll give a bit more on issueA and issueB (low importance) while keeping issueC and issueD high.

The opponent keeps threatening to walk but hasn't actually walked - they're still negotiating. Their bids have been mostly all-valueA with very slow movement (slight concessions on issueD and issueC/issueB occasionally). Their latest offer (all valueA except issueB: valueB) gives me very low utility. We're still very early (t=0.16), so I shouldn't concede too much. My highest priority is issueD (0.4 weight), then issueC (0.3). I should hold firm on issueD and issueC while showing some movement on lower-priority issues. My last bid was {A:valueB, B:valueD, C:valueE, D:valueF}. I'll slightly concede on issueA (least important) while maintaining my positions on issueC and issueD.

The opponent keeps making "final offer" threats but their actual bidding behavior shows very slow concession - they've barely moved (mostly valueA across all issues, with tiny adjustments). Their latest offer at t=0.18 is {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueA}, which gives me utility: 0.1*0.2 + 0.2*0.2 + 0.3*0.52 + 0.4*0.2 = 0.02 + 0.04 + 0.156 + 0.08 = 0.296. That's quite low. I'm still early (t=0.18), so I should continue conceding slowly while keeping high utility on my most important issues (issueD and issueC). I'll try to meet the opponent slightly better on issueA and issueB (where my weights are low) while holding firm on issueC and issueD. My last bid was {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueD, issueC: valueE, issueD: valueF} = 0.1*0.2 + 0.2*0.68 + 0.3*0.84 + 0.4*1.0 = 0.02 + 0.136 + 0.252 + 0.4 = 0.808. I'll maintain strong positioning but make a small concession on issueB to avoid repeating my last bid.

The opponent has been making "final offer" threats every single round but continues to negotiate, so their messages are cheap talk. Their actual bids have been almost entirely at valueA across the board, with occasional small concessions (e.g., issueD: valueB, valueC; issueC: valueB, valueC; issueB: valueB). Their latest offer (t=0.20) gives me utility: 0.1*0.35 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.3*0.20 + 0.4*0.20 = 0.035 + 0.04 + 0.06 + 0.08 = 0.215 — very low. My last bid (issueA: valueA, issueB: valueC, issueC: valueE, issueD: valueF) gives me: 0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.52 + 0.3*0.84 + 0.4*1.0 = 0.02 + 0.104 + 0.252 + 0.4 = 0.776. We're only at t=0.20, so I have plenty of time. I'll hold relatively firm on issueD (highest weight) but make a small concession on issueB to show movement, keeping my utility high.

The opponent keeps bidding all-valueA with minor variations, while constantly threatening to walk away (pure cheap talk since they haven't walked despite 11 rounds). Their latest offer {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueB, issueD: valueC} gives me utility: 0.1×0.2 + 0.2×0.2 + 0.3×0.35 + 0.4×0.52 = 0.02+0.04+0.105+0.208 = 0.373. We're still early (t=0.22), so I should hold firm on my high-weight issues (issueD, issueC) while continuing gradual concessions on lower-weight issues. My current offer {issueA:valueA, issueB:valueC, issueC:valueE, issueD:valueF} has utility 0.1×0.2+0.2×0.52+0.3×0.84+0.4×1.0 = 0.02+0.104+0.252+0.4 = 0.776. I'll concede slightly on issueB (drop to valueB) and keep others, to differentiate from my last bid while maintaining high utility.

The opponent has been consistently bidding near (valueA, valueA, valueA, valueA) with very slight movement - their latest offer at t=0.24 is issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueD, issueD: valueA. Their messages are all aggressive "final offer" threats which are clearly cheap talk since they've continued negotiating for 12 rounds. We're only at t=0.24, still early. My most important issue is issueD (0.4 weight), and I've been holding firm on valueF there. I should continue holding firm on issueD and issueC (my two most important issues) while making small concessions on issueA and issueB. My current bid at t=0.22 was (valueA, valueB, valueE, valueF) with utility = 0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.35 + 0.3*0.84 + 0.4*1.00 = 0.02 + 0.07 + 0.252 + 0.40 = 0.742. I'll make a small concession on issueB by moving to valueC, and try valueE on issueC to maintain value while slightly conceding on issueB.

The opponent keeps making threats but their actual bidding behavior shows slow concessions: they've moved issueC from valueA to valueD, and issueD has flickered between valueA-C. Their latest offer (t=0.26) is {issueA: valueB, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueB, issueD: valueA} which gives me utility = 0.1*0.35 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.3*0.35 + 0.4*0.20 = 0.035+0.04+0.105+0.08 = 0.26. We're still early (t=0.26), so I can hold firm on my highest-value issues. I'll concede slightly on issueA and issueB (low weight) but keep issueC at valueE and issueD at valueF. I'll try a slightly different bid from last time by adjusting issueB to valueB (same as last two turns, so let me change issueA to keep variety).

The opponent keeps making "final offer" threats but is actually slowly conceding - they've moved on issueC from valueA to valueC/valueD and on issueD they've tried valueB and valueC. Their latest offer (t=0.28) is issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueA, which gives me utility: 0.1*0.2 + 0.2*0.35 + 0.3*0.52 + 0.4*0.2 = 0.02 + 0.07 + 0.156 + 0.08 = 0.326 - too low. I'm at t=0.28 which is still early-mid game. My current bid keeps issueD: valueF (most important to me at weight 0.4) and issueC: valueE. I should continue holding firm on issueD (highest weight) while making small concessions elsewhere. I'll keep issueD: valueF and issueC: valueE but slightly adjust issueB to signal cooperation.

The opponent has been consistently offering (valueA, valueA, valueA/B/C/D, valueA/B/C) - their concessions are very slow and their messages are pure bluster ("final offer" repeated 15 times). Their latest offer at t=0.30 is {issueA: valueB, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueA, issueD: valueC}, which gives me utility: 0.1*0.35 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.3*0.20 + 0.4*0.52 = 0.035 + 0.04 + 0.06 + 0.208 = 0.343 - very low. We're only at t=0.3, so I don't need to capitulate yet. I've been holding firm on issueD (valueF) which is my most important issue (weight 0.4). I should continue pressing on issueD while showing some flexibility on lower-weight issues. My current bid at t=0.28 was {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueE, issueD: valueF} with utility = 0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.3*0.84 + 0.4*1.0 = 0.02+0.04+0.252+0.4 = 0.712. I'll try a slight concession on issueD to valueE while keeping issueC at valueD to avoid repeating my last bid.

The opponent's latest offer at t=0.32 is {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueB, issueD: valueE}. Their utility to me: 0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.3*0.35 + 0.4*0.84 = 0.02 + 0.04 + 0.105 + 0.336 = 0.501. That's actually decent progress - they've moved issueD to valueE which is very good for me (weight 0.4). The opponent keeps saying "final offer" but keeps conceding, so I should treat their messages as cheap talk. My current bid targets high utility on issueD and issueC. I should try to find a compromise - I can concede on issueA and issueB (low weight) while holding firm on issueC and issueD. Let me offer something like {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueE, issueD: valueE} = 0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.35 + 0.3*0.84 + 0.4*0.84 = 0.02 + 0.07 + 0.252 + 0.336 = 0.678. This is a meaningful concession from my last bid while maintaining good utility.

The opponent's latest offer at t=0.34 is {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueE, issueD: valueB}. Let me calculate its utility for me: 0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.3*0.84 + 0.4*0.35 = 0.02 + 0.04 + 0.252 + 0.14 = 0.452. That's not great. The opponent has been holding mostly at low values but just moved issueC to valueE (their best move yet) while keeping issueD at valueB. My most important issue is issueD (0.4 weight), so I need to push for higher values there. I should concede on issueA (lowest weight) and make some movement on issueB, while keeping issueC and issueD high. I'll try {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueE, issueD: valueE} which gives utility: 0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.3*0.84 + 0.4*0.84 = 0.02 + 0.04 + 0.252 + 0.336 = 0.648. This is different from my previous bid and shows some movement toward the opponent on issueB while holding issueD high.

The opponent keeps threatening to walk away but has been consistently conceding slowly. Their latest offer at t=0.36 is {issueA: valueB, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueB, issueD: valueA}, which gives me utility: 0.1*0.35 + 0.2*0.35 + 0.3*0.35 + 0.4*0.20 = 0.035 + 0.07 + 0.105 + 0.08 = 0.29 — still low for me. My most important issues are issueD (0.4) and issueC (0.3). The opponent seems to be pushing for low values on issueD (mostly valueA-valueC range). I've been holding firm on issueD: valueF and issueC: valueE/valueF. At t=0.36 I should keep my strong position on issueD and issueC while conceding more on the less important issueA and issueB. I'll try issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueE, issueD: valueE to show movement on issueB while protecting my key issues.

The opponent keeps claiming "final offer" but continues to concede slightly each round - their messages are cheap talk. Their latest offer (t=0.38) is issueA: valueA, issueB: valueC, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueA, which gives me utility: 0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.52 + 0.3*0.52 + 0.4*0.20 = 0.02 + 0.104 + 0.156 + 0.08 = 0.36 - still too low. I need to focus on holding issueD (weight 0.4) and issueC (weight 0.3) at high values. My current offer at t=0.36 was {A:valueA, B:valueA, C:valueE, D:valueE} giving utility 0.02+0.04+0.252+0.336=0.648. I'll hold near this level but make a slight adjustment to show some movement - maybe concede on issueB slightly while maintaining issueD and issueC at high values.

The opponent keeps making "final offer" threats but continues to concede gradually. Their latest offer (t=0.40) is {issueA: valueB, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueB, issueD: valueD}. My utility for that: 0.1*0.35 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.3*0.35 + 0.4*0.68 = 0.035 + 0.04 + 0.105 + 0.272 = 0.452. I'm currently at t=0.4 which is mid-game. My last bid had utility ~0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.35 + 0.3*0.84 + 0.4*0.84 = 0.02 + 0.07 + 0.252 + 0.336 = 0.678. The opponent is slowly improving issueD (went to valueD) and slightly improving issueC. I should keep pressuring on issueD (my highest weight) while making small concessions elsewhere. I'll try offering issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueD, issueD: valueF to maintain high utility on my most important issues.

The opponent's latest offer (issueA: valueB, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueE, issueD: valueA) gives me utility: 0.1*0.35 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.3*0.84 + 0.4*0.20 = 0.035 + 0.04 + 0.252 + 0.08 = 0.407. That's quite low for me. My current best bid pattern has been around issueC: valueE, issueD: valueF/valueE with low values on issueA/issueB. The opponent seems to consistently want low values on issueD (mostly valueA), while I need issueD high (weight 0.4). The opponent just offered issueC: valueE which is valuable to me. I should try to find a compromise - I can give ground on issueA and issueB (low weight for me), but I need to hold on issueD (highest weight). Let me offer something with issueD at valueE, issueC at valueE, and concede issueA/issueB to low values to show flexibility.

The opponent keeps making threats ("final offer", "I walk") but their actual bidding shows gradual concessions - they've moved from all-A offers to occasionally showing valueC, valueD, and even valueE in various issues. Their latest offer (t=0.44) gives issueA: valueC, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueA, which gives me utility: 0.1*0.52 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.3*0.52 + 0.4*0.20 = 0.052 + 0.04 + 0.156 + 0.08 = 0.328 - not great. My current best bids hover around utility 0.84+ for me. At t=0.44 we're still mid-game, so I should continue pressing but start making more meaningful concessions. I'll keep issueD at valueF (highest weight, highest value for me), issueC at valueE, and concede more on issueB (down to valueA) and issueA (valueA). This gives: 0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.3*0.84 + 0.4*1.0 = 0.02 + 0.04 + 0.252 + 0.4 = 0.712. I'll keep this offer similar to recent ones but avoid exact repetition by trying issueB: valueA with issueC: valueF for a different combination.

The opponent's latest offer at t=0.46 is {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueE}. My utility for that would be: 0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.35 + 0.3*0.52 + 0.4*0.84 = 0.02 + 0.07 + 0.156 + 0.336 = 0.582. The opponent keeps threatening to walk but hasn't - their offers are slowly improving (issueD moved to valueE, issueC moved to valueC). My highest priority is issueD (0.4 weight), followed by issueC (0.3). I've been holding firm at issueD: valueF or valueE and issueC: valueE. I'll stay near my current position but make a slight concession on issueA/issueB to show movement, while keeping issueD at valueE and issueC at valueE. My previous bid at t=0.44 was {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueF, issueD: valueE} with utility 0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.3*1.00 + 0.4*0.84 = 0.02 + 0.04 + 0.30 + 0.336 = 0.696. I'll offer something similar but slightly varied.

The opponent has been consistently offering low values (mostly valueA/valueB) across all issues, while their messages are pure bluster ("final offer", "I'll walk"). Their actual behavior shows they ARE conceding slightly over time - issueD went from valueA to valueB/valueC/valueE, and issueC has shown some movement. At t=0.48, their latest offer is {issueA: valueC, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueB}, which gives me utility: 0.1*0.52 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.3*0.52 + 0.4*0.35 = 0.052 + 0.04 + 0.156 + 0.14 = 0.388 - too low for me. My current bids have been around issueC: valueE/valueF and issueD: valueE/valueF which are my most important issues. I should continue holding firm on issueD (weight 0.4) and issueC (weight 0.3) while being flexible on issueA and issueB. I'll offer a slight concession on issueC to valueE and keep issueD at valueE, with issueA/issueB at low values.
